GA360.description: Google Analytics 360 session-level web analytics data from the Google Merchandise Store. Each row represents one user session.

GA360.GOOGLE_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE.GA_SESSION.description: Web analytics sessions from the Google Merchandise Store. Contains session-level metrics, traffic source, device info, geographic data, and per-hit event details including ecommerce transactions.
GA360.GOOGLE_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE.GA_SESSION.partition_info: Daily partitioned as GA_SESSIONS_YYYYMMDD (range: 20160801–20170801). Query one representative table and filter with WHERE "date" >= 'YYYYMMDD' AND "date" < 'YYYYMMDD'.

fullVisitorId.description: The unique visitor ID (primary user identifier). Use this to identify distinct users across sessions.

userId.description: Overridden User ID sent to Analytics (may be null).

visitNumber.description: The session number for this user. First session = 1.

visitId.description: Session identifier. Only unique per user — combine with fullVisitorId for a globally unique session ID.

visitStartTime.description: Session start time as a POSIX/UNIX timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01). Convert with TO_TIMESTAMP(visitStartTime).

date.description: Session date in YYYYMMDD string format. Compare as strings: WHERE "date" >= '20170201' AND "date" < '20170301'.

channelGrouping.description: Default Channel Group for the session (e.g., 'Organic Search', 'Direct', 'Referral', 'Paid Search', 'Social').

totals.description: Session-level aggregate metrics. Access with t."totals":"field"::TYPE. Do NOT LATERAL FLATTEN — this is an object.

fields.hits: INTEGER — Total number of hits in the session.
fields.pageviews: INTEGER — Total pageviews in the session.
fields.transactions: INTEGER — Number of ecommerce transactions. >= 1 means a purchase session.
fields.totalTransactionRevenue: INTEGER — Total transaction revenue multiplied by 10^6. DIVIDE BY 1000000 for USD.
fields.newVisits: INTEGER — 1 if first visit, else null.
fields.visits: INTEGER — 1 for sessions with interaction events, null otherwise.
fields.bounces: INTEGER — 1 if session bounced, else null.

trafficSource.description: Traffic source that originated this session. Access with t."trafficSource":"field"::TYPE.

fields.source: STRING — Traffic source (e.g., 'google', hostname of referrer, or utm_source).
fields.campaign: STRING — Campaign name (usually from utm_campaign URL parameter).
fields.keyword: STRING — Search keyword (when medium is 'organic' or 'cpc', or from utm_term).
fields.isTrueDirect: BOOLEAN — True if session source was direct (user typed URL or used bookmark).
fields.referralPath: STRING — Referral path when medium is 'referral'.
fields.adwordsClickInfo: OBJECT — Nested Google Ads click info (adGroupId, campaignId, gclId, etc.).

device.description: User device information. Access with t."device":"field"::TYPE.

geoNetwork.description: Geographic location of the user based on IP. Access with t."geoNetwork":"field"::TYPE.

hits.description: Array of hits (pageviews, events, transactions) per session. Use LATERAL FLATTEN(input => t."hits") h. Each element has nested objects.
hits.access_pattern: LATERAL FLATTEN(input => t."hits") h, then h.value:"nested":"field"::TYPE

fields.type: STRING — Hit type: PAGE, TRANSACTION, ITEM, EVENT, SOCIAL, APPVIEW, EXCEPTION.

page.pagePath: STRING — URL path of the page.

product._description: OBJECT — Product data. Access: h.value:"product":"v2ProductName"::STRING. NOTE: field is v2ProductName not productName.
product.v2ProductName: STRING — Product name (use this, NOT productName).
product.productRevenue: INTEGER — Product revenue multiplied by 10^6. DIVIDE BY 1000000 for USD.

transaction.transactionRevenue: INTEGER — Transaction revenue multiplied by 10^6. DIVIDE BY 1000000 for USD.

eCommerceAction.action_type: STRING — 1=list click, 2=detail view, 3=add to cart, 4=remove from cart, 5=checkout, 6=completed purchase, 7=refund.

customDimensions.description: Array of session-level custom dimensions. Use LATERAL FLATTEN, then access value:"index" and value:"value".

GA4.description: Google Analytics 4 event-level data from an obfuscated sample ecommerce property. Each row represents one event.

GA4.GA4_OBFUSCATED_SAMPLE_ECOMMERCE.EVEN.description: GA4 event-level analytics data. Each row is one event (page_view, purchase, session_start, etc.). Contains event parameters, item data, user properties, device info, and ecommerce data.
GA4.GA4_OBFUSCATED_SAMPLE_ECOMMERCE.EVEN.partition_info: Daily partitioned as EVENTS_YYYYMMDD (range: 20201101–20210131). Query one representative table and filter with WHERE "event_date" >= 'YYYYMMDD'.

event_date.description: Event date in YYYYMMDD string format. Compare as strings: WHERE "event_date" >= '20201201'.

event_timestamp.description: Event time in microseconds UTC. Convert to timestamp: TO_TIMESTAMP(event_timestamp / 1000000).

event_previous_timestamp.description: Previous event timestamp in microseconds UTC.

event_bundle_sequence_id.description: Sequential ID of the upload bundle.

event_server_timestamp_offset.description: Offset between collection and upload time in microseconds.

user_id.description: Custom user ID assigned to the user.

user_pseudo_id.description: Pseudonymous user ID (app instance ID). Primary user identifier for GA4.

user_first_touch_timestamp.description: Time (microseconds UTC) when user first opened the app or visited the site.

is_active_user.description: Whether the user was active during the calendar day.

stream_id.description: Numeric ID of the data stream.

event_params.description: Array of event parameters (key-value pairs). Use LATERAL FLATTEN, then access value:"key" and value:"value":"string_value" (or int_value, double_value). Common keys: 'page_location', 'page_title', 'page_referrer', 'source', 'medium', 'campaign', 'engagement_time_msec', 'ga_session_id', 'ga_session_number', 'engaged_session_event', 'entrances', 'session_engaged', 'page_view'.

user_properties.description: Array of user properties. Use LATERAL FLATTEN, then access value:"key" and value:"value":"string_value".

fields.value.set_timestamp_micros: INTEGER — When the property was last set.

items.description: Array of items associated with the event (ecommerce). Use LATERAL FLATTEN, then access value:"field". Used in gold SQL: FLATTEN("ITEMS") i, then i.value:"item_id", i.value:"item_name", i.value:"quantity".

fields.price: FLOAT — Item price in local currency.
fields.item_revenue_in_usd: FLOAT — Item revenue in USD (purchase events only).
fields.item_revenue: FLOAT — Item revenue in local currency (purchase events only).
fields.item_list_name: STRING — Name of the list the item was shown in.

ecommerce.description: Ecommerce event data. Access with t."ecommerce":"field"::TYPE. Used in gold SQL: "ECOMMERCE":"transaction_id".

fields.total_item_quantity: INTEGER — Total items in the event.
fields.purchase_revenue_in_usd: FLOAT — Purchase revenue in USD (purchase events only).
fields.purchase_revenue: FLOAT — Purchase revenue in local currency.
fields.refund_value: FLOAT — Refund value in local currency.
fields.shipping_value: FLOAT — Shipping cost in local currency.

geo.description: Geographic location from IP. Access with t."geo":"field"::TYPE.

traffic_source.description: Traffic source that first acquired the user. Access with t."traffic_source":"field"::TYPE.

collected_traffic_source.description: Traffic source collected with the event. Access with t."collected_traffic_source":"field"::TYPE.

user_ltv.description: Lifetime value data. Not populated in intraday tables.

PATENTS.description: Google Patents Public Data on Snowflake. Contains patent publications, classification codes, citations, assignees, and inventors worldwide.

PATENTS.PATENTS.PUBLICATIONS.description: Patent publications worldwide. Each row is one publication. Contains bibliographic data, classification codes (CPC/IPC/USPC), inventors, assignees, citations, and text content. Most VARIANT columns are ARRAYs that require LATERAL FLATTEN.

application_number.description: Patent application number. Multiple publications can share the same application number.

country_code.description: Country of publication (e.g., 'US', 'EP', 'CA', 'DE', 'CN', 'JP'). Filter by this for jurisdiction-specific queries.

kind_code.description: Publication kind: 'A1'=published application, 'A2'=republished application, 'B1'=granted patent (no prior publication), 'B2'=granted patent (with prior A1). 'B2' typically means a granted utility patent.

application_kind.description: Type of application: 'A'=utility, 'F'=design, 'P'=plant. NOTE: This is NOT the patent classification (CPC/IPC). For classification codes use the 'cpc' or 'ipc' VARIANT arrays.

family_id.description: Patent family ID linking related applications across jurisdictions. '-1' means no family assigned.

filing_date.description: Filing date as YYYYMMDD integer (e.g., 20100315). Compare as integers: WHERE "filing_date" >= 20100101 AND "filing_date" <= 20101231. Extract year: FLOOR("filing_date" / 10000).

publication_date.description: Publication date as YYYYMMDD integer. Same format as filing_date.

grant_date.description: Grant date as YYYYMMDD integer. Null if not yet granted.

priority_date.description: Earliest priority date as YYYYMMDD integer.

cpc.description: Array of CPC (Cooperative Patent Classification) codes. Use LATERAL FLATTEN(input => p."cpc") c. Patent category queries (e.g., 'A61' for medical) filter on c.value:"code"::STRING LIKE 'A61%'. Gold SQL confirms this pattern.

fields.code: STRING — Full CPC code (e.g., 'A61K31/00'). Use SUBSTRING(code, 1, 4) for 4-digit level.
fields.first: BOOLEAN — True if this is the primary/main CPC code for the patent.
fields.inventive: BOOLEAN — True if this code relates to the inventive aspect.

ipc.description: Array of IPC (International Patent Classification) codes. Same structure as cpc. Use LATERAL FLATTEN + value:"code"::STRING.

fields.first: BOOLEAN — True if main IPC code.

uspc.description: Array of USPC (US Patent Classification) codes. Same structure as cpc/ipc.

fi.description: Array of FI (Japanese File Index) classification codes.

inventor.description: Array of inventor names as simple strings. Use LATERAL FLATTEN(input => p."inventor") i, then i.value::STRING for the name. Count inventors per patent with COUNT(DISTINCT i.index).

inventor_harmonized.description: Array of harmonized inventor records. Use LATERAL FLATTEN, then value:"name"::STRING and value:"country_code"::STRING.

assignee.description: Array of assignee (patent owner) names as simple strings. Use LATERAL FLATTEN(input => p."assignee") a, then a.value::STRING. Used as fallback when assignee_harmonized is empty.

assignee_harmonized.description: Array of harmonized assignee records. Preferred over 'assignee' for analysis. Use LATERAL FLATTEN, then UPPER(TRIM(ah.value:"name"::STRING)) for normalized names. Gold SQL uses this pattern extensively.

citation.description: Array of patent citations (both backward and forward). Use LATERAL FLATTEN(input => p."citation") c. Gold SQL pattern: c.value:publication_number::STRING to get cited patent numbers.

fields.filing_date: NUMBER — Filing date of cited patent (YYYYMMDD integer).

abstract_localized.description: Array of localized abstracts. Use LATERAL FLATTEN, filter by value:"language"::STRING = 'en', access text with value:"text"::STRING. Gold SQL uses LOWER(abs.value:"text"::STRING) LIKE '%keyword%' for text search.

title_localized.description: Array of localized titles. Same structure as abstract_localized.

claims_localized.description: Array of localized patent claims. Gold SQL uses FLATTEN + value:"text"::STRING with REGEXP_LIKE for keyword search in claims.

parent.description: Array of parent patent relationships (continuations, divisionals).

PATENTS.PATENTS.DISCLOSURES_13.description: Standard Essential Patent (SEP) disclosure data from the 13 major Standard Setting Organizations (SSOs). Links patents to technical standards.

family_id.description: Patent family ID, links to PUBLICATIONS.family_id.

pub_cleaned.description: Cleaned publication number. Can be matched to PUBLICATIONS.publication_number.

PATENTS.PATENTS.CPC_DEFINITION.description: CPC (Cooperative Patent Classification) hierarchy definitions. Each row defines one CPC symbol with its title, relationships, and references.

titleFull.description: Full human-readable title of the CPC symbol.

notAllocatable.description: True if this symbol cannot be directly assigned to patents.

breakdownCode.description: True if this is a breakdown code.

applicationReferences.description: Application references (VARIANT ARRAY with fields: description, target).

PATENTS_GOOGLE.description: Google Patents Research data including text embeddings, similarity scores, and enriched patent metadata.

PATENTS_GOOGLE.PATENTS_GOOGLE.PUBLICATIO.description: Patent publications with the same structure as PATENTS.PATENTS.PUBLICATIONS but in the PATENTS_GOOGLE database context. Contains publication_number, filing_date, grant_date, country_code, kind_code, family_id, cpc, ipc, assignee_harmonized, citation, etc.

cpc.description: ARRAY of CPC codes. Use LATERAL FLATTEN + value:"code"::STRING. Same structure as PATENTS.PUBLICATIONS.cpc.

citation.description: ARRAY of citations. value:publication_number::STRING for cited patent.

PATENTS_GOOGLE.PATENTS_GOOGLE.ABS_AND_EM.description: Patent abstracts with text embeddings for similarity computation. Contains pre-computed embedding vectors and similar patent lists.

publication_number.description: Publication number. Join with PUBLICATIONS on this column.

top_terms.description: Top terms extracted from the patent.

similar.description: ARRAY of similar patents with similarity scores. Use LATERAL FLATTEN.

embedding_v1.description: Text embedding vector (ARRAY of FLOATs). Use LATERAL FLATTEN to access individual dimensions. Compute similarity between patents using dot product of their embedding vectors. Gold SQL: FLATTEN(embedding_v1) f, then f.value::FLOAT.

cited_by.description: ARRAY of patents that cite this patent (forward citations).

PATENTS_GOOGLE.PATENTS_GOOGLE.CPC_DEFINI.description: Same structure as PATENTS.PATENTS.CPC_DEFINITION. CPC hierarchy definitions.

PATENTS_GOOGLE.PATENTS_GOOGLE.DISCLOSURE.description: Same structure as PATENTS.PATENTS.DISCLOSURES_13. SEP disclosure data.
